papers.title: Test-Time Adaptation for EEG Foundation Models: A Systematic Study under Real-World Distribution Shifts
papers.abstract: Abstract Test-time adaptation methods for EEG foundation models show inconsistent performance across distribution shifts, with optimization-free approaches demonstrating greater stability than gradient-based methods.  AI-generated summary Electroencephalography (EEG) foundation models have shown strong potential for learning generalizable representations from large-scale neural data, yet their clinical deployment is hindered by distribution shifts across clinical settings, devices, and populations. Test-time adaptation (TTA) offers a promising solution by enabling models to adapt to unlabeled target data during inference without access to source data, a valuable property in healthcare settings constrained by privacy regulations and limited labeled data. However, its effectiveness for EEG remains largely underexplored. In this work, we introduce NeuroAdapt-Bench, a systematic benchmark for evaluating test-time adaptation methods on EEG foundation models under realistic distribution shifts. We evaluate representative TTA approaches from other domains across multiple pretrained foundation models, diverse downstream tasks, and heterogeneous datasets spanning in-distribution, out-of-distribution, and extreme modality shifts (e.g., Ear-EEG). Our results show that standard TTA methods yield inconsistent gains and often degrade performance, with gradient-based approaches particularly prone to heavy degradation. In contrast, optimization-free methods demonstrate greater stability and more reliable improvements. These findings highlight the limitations of existing TTA techniques in EEG, provide guidance for future development, and underscore the need for domain-specific adaptation strategies.

ru.title: Адаптация без оптимизации: стабильный путь для EEG моделей при распределительных сдвигах
ru.desc: В работе представлен NeuroAdapt-Bench — первый систематический бенчмарк для оценки методов адаптации на этапе тестирования (TTA) на основе фундаментальных моделей EEG. Исследователи протестировали различные подходы TTA из других областей машинного обучения на множестве предобученных моделей, задач и наборов данных с распределения, включая экстремальные сдвиги модальностей. Результаты показали, что градиентные методы адаптации часто приводят к деградации производительности, тогда как оптимизационно-свободные методы демонстрируют большую стабильность и надежные улучшения. Выводы подчеркивают необходимость разработки специализированных стратегий адаптации, учитывающих особенности EEG-данных в клинических условиях.

en.title: Stability in EEG Adaptation: Optimize Without Gradients!
en.desc: This paper discusses the challenges of using test-time adaptation (TTA) methods for EEG foundation models, particularly when faced with distribution shifts in clinical settings. It highlights that while TTA can help models adapt to new, unlabeled data during inference, many existing methods show inconsistent performance, especially gradient-based approaches that often degrade results. The authors introduce NeuroAdapt-Bench, a benchmark designed to evaluate various TTA methods on EEG models under realistic conditions. Their findings suggest that optimization-free methods are more stable and effective, indicating a need for tailored adaptation strategies in the EEG domain.

zh.title: 提升脑电图模型稳定性的关键在于无优化方法
zh.desc: 本研究探讨了在脑电图（EEG）基础模型中，测试时适应（TTA）方法在分布变化下的表现。我们发现，无优化的方法比基于梯度的方法更稳定，后者在性能上常常出现下降。为此，我们提出了NeuroAdapt-Bench，这是一个系统的基准测试，用于评估EEG基础模型在真实分布变化下的TTA方法。我们的结果表明，现有的TTA技术在EEG领域存在局限性，未来需要开发特定领域的适应策略。
